reforms.description: Greece has enacted significant reforms to improve the governance of universities and the tertiary education system, focusing on independent evaluation and linking funding to performance evaluations. These reforms include moving to a four-year budgeting cycle and establishing an evaluation system for universities to enhance accountability and performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving governance and funding mechanisms in universities is expected to enhance the quality of education and research output, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in the governance and funding of higher education.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently enacted a reform, which includes a series of important measures which will improve the governance of universities.

reforms.description: A new Digital Strategy will be implemented over the period 2006-13, aiming to improve support for research and development (R&D) innovation programmes and increase R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to foster innovation and enhance the competitiveness of the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been announced and is set to be implemented over the specified period.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Strategy represents a major commitment to enhancing R&D funding and support.
reforms.source_quote: A new Digital Strategy will be implemented over the period 2006-13.

reforms.description: The government has established a legal framework for public-private partnerships (PPPs) to enhance the efficiency of public investment, allowing for cost-sharing between the state and private entities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the efficiency of public investment through PPPs is expected to improve service delivery and stimulate economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The legal framework for PPPs has been established and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a legal framework for PPPs represents a significant reform in public investment strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The 2005 legislation established a legal framework for public-private partnerships (PPPs) and is a welcome step towards increasing the efficiency of public investment.

reforms.description: The Greek government is implementing reforms to modernize the health care system, including the introduction of an integrated information system for better management and efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Modernizing the health care system is expected to improve efficiency and service delivery, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been announced and are set to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant but narrower change in the health care system.
reforms.source_quote: Ongoing efforts to reform health care aim at modernising the sector and improving its efficiency.

reforms.description: The Greek government plans to apply public-private partnerships in the health sector to enhance service provision and operational efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Utilizing PPPs in the health sector is expected to improve service delivery and operational efficiency, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans for applying PPPs in the health sector have been announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant but narrower change in the health sector.
reforms.source_quote: The application of public-private partnerships to the health sector is planned for the provision of auxiliary services.
